title: Validation of an LC-MS/MS method for the quantitation of phytosterols derived from Aloe vera gel
author: Nabeshima, Mizutani, et al.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: The solutions of calibration standard (10, 20, 40, 80, and 160 ng/mL) were used to build the calibration curve and its coefficient of determination was calculated.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Calculation of the limit of quantification and relative standard deviation (RSD)

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: The calibration standard (10 ng/mL) was analyzed five times to calculate the standard deviation, limit of quantification (10 S/N) [12], and RSD.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: A recovery test was performed to confirm the accuracy of this method. Recovery was calculated as follows:

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: Recovery (%) = (C spiked – C sample) / C standard × 100

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: C spiked: concentration of the sample to be spiked

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: C sample: concentration of the sample

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: C standard: concentration of the standard

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: The method precision was assessed by repeatability and intermediate precision. The repeatability was calculated by measuring the same sample 5 times on the same day. The intermediate precision was determined by the results of samples (n = 2 /day) prepared and analyzed on six different days. The RSDs were calculated.

steps.title: LC-MS/MS analysis
steps.instruction: Liquid chromatography was performed using the Agilent Technology 1260 system equipped with a degasser, binary pump, multisampler, and column compartment. Chromatographic separation was performed on an Ascentis Express C18 HPLC column (2.1 × 100 mm, 2.7 μm particles; Sigma-Aldrich Supelco) with a guard column (2.1 × 5 mm, 2.7 μm particles; Sigma-Aldrich Supelco). The analytic conditions were as follows: injection volume, 10 μL; flow rate, 0.4 mL/min; column temperature, 50 °C; multisampler, 10 °C. Solvent A was acetonitrile/water (90:10 v/v) and solvent B was methanol/water/formic acid (900:100:1, v/v/v). The gradient program was as follows: solvent A 100%, solvent B 0% (0–8.5 min), solvent A 0%, solvent B 100% (8.5–24 min), and solvent A 100%, solvent B 0% (24–36 min).

steps.title: LC-MS/MS analysis
steps.instruction: For MS/MS, we used Agilent Technology 6460 Triple Quad LC/MS. The parameters are as follows: nebulizer pressure, 20 psi; drying gas flow rate, 4 mL/min; temperature, 325 °C; capillary voltage, 4,500 V; collision gas, N2. The other parameters are shown in Table 1. Chromatograms of the standard solution and a measurement sample are shown in Fig. 2.Table 1MS/MS parameters of Aloe sterols.Table 1Compoundprecursor ion(m/z)product ion (m/z)fragmentor voltage (V)Collision energy(V)polaritylophenol383.5301.312015+24-methyl-lophenol397.4315.418015+24-ethyl-lophenol411.6411.617015+cycloartanol429.8191.111015+24-methylene-cycloartanol441.1191.416018+Fig. 2Chromatograms of (A) 160 ng/mL standard solution, (B) measurement sample.Fig 2

steps.title: Sample preparation
steps.instruction: AVGP, a fine powder from dried and ground Aloe vera mesophyll, was used as a sample for the validation of the developed quantitative method. For the sample preparation, AVGP (1 g) was added to a 50 mL flask, followed by 40 mL of the extraction solvent (chloroform/methanol (2:1, v/v)). This mixture was dissolved by sonication for 10 min at 25 °C. The volume was then adjusted to 50 mL with the extraction solvent. The sample suspension (5 mL) was centrifuged (1150 × g, 25 °C, 5 min) and the supernatant (2 mL) was collected. The solvent was eliminated by evaporation at 40 °C under a nitrogen stream. The dried residue was dissolved in 20 mL of methanol in ambience by sonication for 15 min and filtered through a 0.22 μm filter (PVDF, Tomsic, Japan) to obtain a measurement sample.
